usage: Copy any payload object into the backend /debug Trip Input box, then click Run Trip.

cases.title: Basic Smoke Test
cases.purpose: Simple balanced trip to confirm the workflow runs from trip creation to HITL to final itinerary.
watch_for: 5 destination candidates
successful city confirmation
non-empty trip pitch
3-day itinerary with rationale and constraint_notes

payload.group_notes: Keep the trip relaxed, avoid rushed mornings, and make sure the plan respects everyone's must-haves.

members.name: Alice
members.preference_notes: Hates early mornings, no clubs, wants Italian food at least once.

members.name: Bob
members.preference_notes: Likes relaxed days with good meals and urban neighborhoods, but does not want an overpacked schedule.

members.name: Carol
members.preference_notes: Interested in scenic walks and museums, but not late-night clubbing.

cases.title: Preference Weight Conflict
cases.purpose: Tests how the workflow handles numeric preference conflicts without strong natural-language constraints.
watch_for: preference_conflicts should mention spread between members
destination candidates should balance outdoor, urban, nightlife, and shopping tension
rationale should explain compromise choices

payload.group_notes: The group is split on what kind of trip this should be. Try to find a fair compromise.

members.name: Dana
members.preference_notes: Happy with whatever the group picks.

members.name: Eli
members.preference_notes: Wants city energy and iconic neighborhoods.

members.name: Mina
members.preference_notes: Would love boutiques, markets, and good restaurants.

members.name: Gabe
members.preference_notes: Mostly wants scenic walks and not too much nightlife.

cases.title: Natural-Language Preference Conflict
cases.purpose: Tests conflicts expressed in preference_notes and group_notes, especially hard constraints versus soft wishes.
watch_for: preference_constraints should extract no clubs, no early mornings, seafood, museums, and packed/relaxed conflict
dynamic_tool_selection should avoid nightlife if hard-avoided
constraint_notes should explain which constraints were respected

payload.group_notes: This group has conflicting notes: some want a packed city trip, others want slow mornings and no loud nightlife. Hard constraints should win.

members.name: Nora
members.preference_notes: No clubs or bars. I hate early mornings. I need gluten-free meals.

members.name: Ryan
members.preference_notes: I want the schedule packed, with museums, food halls, and late-night energy.

members.name: Sofia
members.preference_notes: I want seafood restaurants avoided because of my shellfish allergy. I prefer art museums and scenic neighborhoods.

cases.title: Budget Limit Crossing
cases.purpose: Tests severe or moderate budget pressure, destination retries, hotel ceiling behavior, and whether the output explains budget compromise.
watch_for: budget_status should be moderate or severe
decision_log should show budget analysis and possible destination retry
hotel choice should respect ceiling when possible
rationale should mention lower-cost choices when relevant

payload.group_notes: This must be as cheap as possible. Avoid expensive hotels, paid tours, and high-cost dining.

members.name: Sam
members.preference_notes: Free outdoor activities are best. I cannot go over budget.

members.name: Maya
members.preference_notes: Simple food, walkable neighborhoods, and no costly attractions.

members.name: Leo
members.preference_notes: I want nature but the budget matters more than the destination.

cases.title: Food Restrictions and Schedule Feasibility
cases.purpose: Tests vegan, halal, gluten-free validation, late-start constraints, relaxed pace, and day-level constraint notes.
watch_for: meals should avoid restricted keywords
schedule should avoid non-breakfast items before 10:00
constraint_satisfaction should pass or explain unmet constraints
constraint_notes should mention food restrictions

payload.group_notes: Food restrictions are mandatory. Keep mornings slow and avoid long travel between activities.

members.name: Nina
members.preference_notes: No dairy, eggs, meat, wheat, pasta, or bread. I want relaxed mornings.

members.name: Omar
members.preference_notes: No pork or alcohol-centered places. Museums and scenic walks are ideal.

members.name: Priya
members.preference_notes: Vegetarian meals only. I like parks, cafes, and calm evenings.

cases.title: Many Origins and Flight Fairness
cases.purpose: Tests flight fetch/fallback across far-apart origins, budget fairness, and whether the itinerary remains reasonable after flight-time alignment.
watch_for: one flight per member
align_flight_times should trim day 1 or final day if needed
fairness_scores and compatibility_scores should be populated
decision_log should include fairness outcome

payload.group_notes: People are flying from far apart. Keep the first day light and avoid assuming everyone arrives early.

members.name: Ava
members.preference_notes: I like parks and markets, but first day should be easy.

members.name: Ben
members.preference_notes: Urban neighborhoods and good food are the priority.

members.name: Chloe
members.preference_notes: Museums, walking, and no exhausting travel days.

members.name: Diego
members.preference_notes: Keep costs fair across the group.

cases.title: Long Six-Member High-Conflict Trip
cases.purpose: Stress test: six travelers, five nights, conflicting weights, conflicting natural language, dietary restrictions, budget spread, and explainability.
watch_for: preference_conflicts should be non-empty
natural-language constraints should extract hard avoids and soft wishes
dynamic tools should include/exclude categories based on both weights and hard constraints
itinerary should cover the full date range
rationale should explain compromises day by day
constraint_notes should show which hard constraints were honored
fairness may pass or fail, but the decision_log should explain why

payload.group_notes: This is a reunion trip with conflicting styles. Hard constraints: no clubs, no alcohol-focused venues, no pork, respect vegan and gluten-free meals, no activity before 10:00 unless it is breakfast. Soft goals: one scenic outdoor day, one museum/culture day, one food-focused day, one shopping or market stop, and enough downtime that the trip does not feel rushed.

members.name: Alex
members.preference_notes: I want hiking, viewpoints, and nature. I am vegan. I hate clubs and do not want alcohol-centered venues.

members.name: Brooke
members.preference_notes: I want museums, architecture, cafes, and iconic city neighborhoods. I do not care about hiking.

members.name: Cam
members.preference_notes: I am budget sensitive and gluten-free. No packed schedule. I need downtime every day.

members.name: Dev
members.preference_notes: No pork or bacon. I like late dinners, markets, and food halls, but not clubs.

members.name: Ella
members.preference_notes: I want boutiques, local markets, photogenic streets, and at least one nicer dinner.

members.name: Finn
members.preference_notes: I want beaches or water if possible, but I cannot handle early mornings. Keep travel between stops low.

cases.title: Big City Skyscraper Intent
cases.purpose: Regression test for urban/skyscraper language so popular major cities are included instead of only niche destinations.
watch_for: destination_intent should include big_city, skyscrapers, skyline, museums, or nightlife_city
candidate destinations should include at least two major cities
candidate list should include at least one iconic city like Chicago, New York City, San Francisco, Boston, Los Angeles, Seattle, or Miami
candidate reasoning should mention skyline, skyscrapers, museums, nightlife, food, or dense urban exploration

payload.group_notes: People are flying from far apart. Keep the first day light and avoid assuming everyone arrives early.

members.name: Ava
members.preference_notes: I like big cities and urban exploration and want great nightclubs, but first day should be easy.

members.name: Ben
members.preference_notes: Urban neighborhoods with dense skyscrapers and good food are the priority.

members.name: Chloe
members.preference_notes: Museums, walking, and no exhausting travel days. Also prefer a city with tall skyscrapers.

members.name: Diego
members.preference_notes: Keep costs fair across the group.
